column27: Comments

Game 1 was not very fun. It was so lopsided. On turn 5 I hit Tomes 4 times (see discord for how). Second game was much more fun. Dr. Voodoo is great. I chose she hulk and Peter's Allies to help with the astral plane. In my winning game did so with just 4 cards and a very lucky villain deck. Only saw one scheme twist and no masterstrikes.

This round was very much about the luck of the draw from the villain's deck. First round hit a few Master Strike & Scheme Twists early, took a long time to finally win. Second round only hit 3 Scheme Twists.

Kylah built a Spider-Friends deck using Allies & High Tech. Ollie played around with Rick Jones & Happy. Hero choices were difficult this week - we tossed around the idea a bit trying to decide if we wanted to switch it up after our first game.  First game was decent, but felt it still had potential. Danger sense definitely was the MVP allowing us to bring out the villains we wanted and avoided twists and strikes.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27:
I knew going into the setup I was going to have to be quick and lucky, but after seeing other people’s comments I knew I had my work cut out for me.

It was super easy to get recruit to focus towards the MM and the city to keep it clear. I know my turns could’ve been lower, but I felt good about this play through. Overall fun, but too easy to be a 5/5.
2nd attempt was a lot easier because I didn't draw scheme twists and masters strikes on the first six turns. Also the HQ wasn't completely clogged with Heads of Security like the 1st attempt.

Groot+Peter's allies worked great together. Groots rare letting you use shards as stars instead and michelle letting you fight with stars was a great combo. I would have won quicker but I hit 2 master strikes extremely early

Felt that this was a slow win for a low stat mastermind, got bogged down early in both games with high cost cards in the HQ used drax, hogan and Hirom to power through the attack side of things while going all out for stars on the other to lower the masterminds shard count and astral plains villains.
 Don’t think this result will do well but hopefully carry some weight.

I feel like this took me too many turns to defeat.  My first game was about 8 turns faster but had too many escape right away.  Second attempt, had too many early master strikes and it slowed the game down considerably.

Ancient One and Peter's Allies significantly helped with buying shards from Toomes and handling the Fear Lords, as well as enhancing Coordinate synergy with Happy Hogan. First game went extremely well, thought I might be able to top it in the second but it ended up being a trainwreck. But the first game went as good as I could've hoped considering the shuffle, very strong pair.

Our first game didn't go well with the spider men. Toomes ended up getting very early strikes which made him unhittable for most of the game until the villain deck ran out. Our 2nd game went much better. We were able to get all possible VP up to when we finally won the game. Hoping it's fast enough. Overall pretty fun setup.

column2: Baron Helmut Zemo's Demon Bear Saga
column27: Really hard to find a good combo of heroes given the starting trio.
I love Scarlett witch so glad I got to play with her.

Brought Karma and Cloak & Dagger to keep the hero deck entirely covert and ranged. This made all superpowers super easy to trigger. I(snash108) was primarily ranged, while my wife focused on covert. 

Probably would have gone 4-5 turns shorter if Silver Surfer hadn't decided to go on vacation...

Not sure if this is good enough to move on, but man was it fun. An all blue and red hero deck is definitely a good time. Got to cancel two master strikes with Speedball's rare and also got to destroy two city spaces with Galactus. Never had the demon bear escape, which gave me tons of extra bystanders. Probably too many turns to move on but my PPT were excellent so I hope that was enough. GG to you all!

Maybe I took the bait here and selected blue and red hero sets, not sure if it was the best plan. Attempt 2 was going so well when I picked up Penumbra on turn 8, but then Galactus Hungers got Chaos Magic'd to the bottom of the deck when I could have easily recruited it. Took me until turn 40 to win on that one.  Attempt 1 became the selection after that. Speedball and Scarlet Witch just don’t work well in my hands.

I submitted my first attempt for it was much faster and I had more VP/Turn. But those two escapes could be my end here. My start was pretty bad with two early escapes but I hit the Demon Bear many times in the city. So I grabbed many Bystanders :) One hand focused on 2-cost-cards with Peter Parker, the 2-cost Storm&Black Panther and Scarlet Witch while the other hand did an excellent job with the Heralds & Speedball. In my second match I replaced Peter Parker with Cloak & Dagger. But the match took too long. 37 turns but 0 Escapes here.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Zemo came out swinging and delivered several wounds to each hand in the first few turns. This really slowed the game down, and then Galactus Hungers and a bunch of 6-cost heroes were just hanging out in the HQ! Eventually right hand had a good dark memories deck and right hand had Galactus and things got going, but boy that was a slog!
We went all in with blue/red, and had a blast. We ate so many wounds though. Luckily we killed both radioactive men in the first 6 turns or so.

Could not get anything going. Felt like I had 100 shield agents in both decks. Heros were clunky and hard to work. Didn't have very good options for K.O power so wounds were a problem. Not being familiar with any of the heroes didn't help either. Overall not a very fun pod even though I did win the second game it still felt like a loss.
Great setup.  First game 6 scheme twists in 10 turns and it was over.  Second game ran out the villain deck.  We handled the bear just fine but zemo was just a bit too tough for our decks.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: It felt kinda slow, but i was (barely) able to avoid escapes. And then Guardian's rare went nutso and finished it.

Easily the most fun setup of the tournament so far, and probably in my top 5 setups of all time. Yelena overperformed in combination with Red Guardian. Stepford Cuckoos and Warpath put in work. The thing I loved about this set up is every turn I was either drawing cards, investigating, unleashing, making tough decisions. Having to play my cards in a specific order so I could hit taskmaster, or pull off a clone trigger correctly. Incredibly fun, I'll be stashing this one and playing it again for sure

On my first game I saw how strong covert would be with Red Guardian in the mix, so on the second attempt I used two covert-centric heroes with Ms. Marvel and Siryn. It was a great choice, as both sides just focused on making covert decks. Both sides got to be pretty strong at the end, and thinned down due to the bridge builders and bringing them back when I could to get even more KO. Luckily, the Henchman were only 3 attack for most of the game, and I only ended up drawing 2 Master Strikes in total. No super crazy turns, just very consistent on both sides to keep the Henchmen in check until I could focus on Taskmaster.
This setup is masterpiece. Had to go full "Red deck wins" and cheer whole lot of fans to the bottom of stack. Also Epic Sidera Maris with 13 shards

Excellent setup, I'll be keeping it in mind for future plays.  Also first time playing Red Guardian and Yelena together and they are perfect together.  The other deck stacked Cuckoos and took care of Taskmaster with only +2's and 3's.

Stupid Clones gave me one Escape. Otherwise a good 2nd match. Shards are a nice thing to get rid of the Taskmaster, so I chose Phyla-Vell & Ronan. Too bad I didn't get multiple copies of Rally Kree Warriors in my both attempts. I also used Cheering Crowds quite often so that made me lose some important VP.

Very challenging but rewarding setup. First time all tournament I changed a Hero from play 1 to 2, and ultimately the switch to Phylla allowed everything to run smoother, including hilariously delivering the Final Blow without playing any cards- just 5 Shards.
Everybody watch Taskmaster (UK). That's all I have to say

Whew, tough one. The villain synergy almost guaranteed escapes. Danger sense was ok but it didn't mitigate early strikes so henchmen were a pain. I though that leaning in on Champions would help in round 2 but it ended up being worst and a jester absolutely wrecked my game 2.

column2: Maximus' Symbiotic Absorption of The Goblin
column27: Very long turns, and longer games than usual. Investigate was key to our wins, very nice keyword.

Brought Captain Britain, to minimize printed attack available for Abomination. Chose Agent Venom for similar reasons. Also helped bring another hero group to power up Captain Britain even quicker.

Went pretty well. First game dragged a bit too long for my liking, but in the 2nd game was able to score 3 rares(Carnage's, Daredevil Noir's and Medusa's). Got my decks super thin, and hit Maximus multiple times in the last few turns, which is when I know I've got a solid deck built!!

I almost put some respect on Angel Noir's name, but then we played the second game with Agent Venom. Edited was able to recruit Carnage's rare and used it to KO 8 0 cost cards in one turn. We then proceeded to hit the MM all 9 times before the poison armor card came out.
Venom and Carnage for the win.

Attempt 1 Captain Britain helped get Colossus’ rare on turn 3, and had a decent but kind of slightly slow win. I had high hopes for attempt 2 with Elsa and Arkon when I saw Carnage’s rare in the starting HQ, but despite my best efforts to buy Officers to ensure I got it early it kept eluding me and then when I did get it it kept hitting non-zero cards and only once I think did it KO more than one card, so this attempt finished up only marginally quicker than the first. Submitted attempt 2 because I thought an extra turn might be worth more than ~0.1 difference in PPT.

Our first game went really well with Venompool's commons and Karnak's Uncommons pulling most of the weight in Grey's deck while Blue ran with Medusa and Carnage mostly. While there were no escapes in the first game, the second game was a couple turns shorter and had more points, so we went with that score instead. The second game Grey murdered the board by landing all 3 of Venompool's Uncommons and the Rare. Was rescuing so many bystanders, boosting attack, and quadrupling effects. (Luckily Amy can't be duplicated....) Super fun game having Maximus use someone else's tactics.

I decided not to spend ages picking Heroes, so I jumped with Gorgon and Karnak to lean into the "When Recruited" abilities to hopefully give a little speed and flexibility. I'm not sure it totally worked; sometimes I had to resist buying a decent Hero because the timing meant I'd "waste" the When Recruited ability. But there were definitely a few good combo moments. I'd hoped maybe to get Karnak's rare so the When Recruited abilities could be re-used, but it never came out. Oh, I did also steer clear of any Heroes with especially large printed Attack so as not to pump up Maximus's "Highest Abomination", and that did work out. Unfortunately I still got Carnage's 6-attack Rare 

I expected them to be long games since we'd likely have to hit the Mastermind at least 9 times, and they were. I was mostly able to time attacking Symbiotes to prevent them from Bonding, and generally keeping the City clear was straightforward; it was just a matter of getting 11-14 attack as many times as possible. I did starve myself of Recruit a bit both by culling Shield Agents and in my Hero selections, which occasionally meant Hidden Witnesses gummed up the works. In the end, both games were very long, but the slightly longer one had much higher points-per-turn, so I'm submitting that.

MVP honestly was probably Detective Wolverine, as he Investigated *two* Scheme Twists to the bottom of the Villain deck in Game 1 and assured me that at least I wouldn't have an early loss. A similar event in Game 2 let me put one Scheme Twist down there, which certainly made things less stressful. Investigate can be a crazy powerful keyword.

havok/ wonder man on one side , lightshow on the other side, worked but took some time, scheme twist shoved 3 Greithoth out before we could wind up
This one was pretty easy. X-men just rolled over everyone. Shadow king went home crying.
